metadata.summary: A toolkit for WeatherBench based on PyTorch

documentation:
# wxbtool

[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/269931312.svg)](https://zenodo.org/badge/latestdoi/269931312)

A toolkit for WeatherBench based on PyTorch

Install
--------

```bash
pip install wxbtool
```

Cheat sheet
-----------

Start a data set server for 3-days prediction of t850 by Weyn's solution
```bash
wxb dserve -m wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850weyn -s Setting3d
```

Start a training process for a UNet model following Weyn's solution
```bash
wxb train -m wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3sm_weyn
```

Start a testing process for a UNet model following Weyn's solution
```bash
wxb test -m wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3sm_weyn
```

Start a data set server with http binding
```bash
wxb dserve -m wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850weyn -s Setting3d 
```

Start a data set server with ip and port
```bash
wxb dserve -m wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850weyn -s Setting3d -b 127.0.0.1:8088
```

Start a training process with unix socket binding
```bash
wxb train -m wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3sm_weyn -d unix:/tmp/test.sock
```

How to use
-----------

* [quick start](https://github.com/caiyunapp/wxbtool/wiki/quick-start)
* understanding the physical process by plotting
* develop your own neural model
* try a toy physical model
* explore the possibility to combine neural and physical model together

How to release
---------------

```bash
uv build
uv publish

git tag va.b.c master
git push origin va.b.c
```

Contributors
------------

* Mingli Yuan ([Mountain](https://github.com/mountain))
* Ren Lu

modules.name: wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3hg_rasp
documentation:
Demo model in wxbtool package

This model predict t850 3-days in the future
it cost more memory and can be fitted into one P40 gpu at batch size 4
the weighted rmse is ??? K

modules.name: wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3bg_weyn
documentation:
Demo model in wxbtool package

This model predict t850 3-days in the future
it cost more memory and can be fitted into one P40 gpu at batch size 64
the weighted rmse is 2.23 K

modules.name: wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3sm_weyn
documentation:
Demo model in wxbtool package

This model predict t850 3-days in the future
The performance is relative weak, but it can be easily fitted into one normal gpu
the weighted rmse is 2.41 K

modules.name: wxbtool.zoo.res5_625.unet.t850d3sm_rasp
documentation:

This model predict t850 3-days in the future
The performance is relative weak, but it can be easily fitted into one normal gpu
the weighted rmse is 2.12 K

it cost more memory and can be fitted into one P40 gpu at batch size 8

class_variables.name: wxbtool.data.download.Config.retention_days
documentation: Number of days to retain data

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.ensure_output_dirs
documentation: Create the base directories for each variable.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.get_time_span
documentation: Determine the start and end dates based on the coverage type.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.generate_datetime_list
documentation: Generate a list of (date, hour) tuples for each hour within the time span.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.build_filename
documentation: Construct the filename for the NetCDF file.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.ensure_variable_dirs
documentation: Ensure that the directory for a specific variable, year, and month exists.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.retrieve_data
documentation: Retrieve data for a specific variable, date, and time.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.manage_retention
documentation: Manage data retention by deleting or archiving old files.

methods.name: wxbtool.data.download.ERA5Downloader.download
documentation: Execute the download process based on the configuration.

modules.name: wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850rasp
documentation:
A modeling spec for t850

This spec follows basic settings and discussions in

  Data-driven medium-range weather prediction with a Resnet pretrained on climate simulations:
  A new model for WeatherBench
  by Stephan Rasp, Nils Thuerey
  https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08626.pdf

modules.name: wxbtool.specs.res5_625.z500weyn
documentation:
A modeling spec for z500

This spec follows basic settings and discussions in

  Improving Data‐Driven Global Weather Prediction Using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks on a Cubed Sphere
  by Jonathan A. Weyn, Dale R. Durran, Rich Caruana
  https://doi.org/10.1029/2020MS002109

modules.name: wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850weyn
documentation:
A modeling spec for t850

This spec follows basic settings and discussions in

  Improving Data‐Driven Global Weather Prediction Using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks on a Cubed Sphere
  by Jonathan A. Weyn, Dale R. Durran, Rich Caruana
  https://doi.org/10.1029/2020MS002109

modules.name: wxbtool.specs.res5_625.t850recur
documentation:
A modeling spec for t850

This spec follows basic settings and discussions in

  Improving Data‐Driven Global Weather Prediction Using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks on a Cubed Sphere
  by Jonathan A. Weyn, Dale R. Durran, Rich Caruana
  https://doi.org/10.1029/2020MS002109

But specialized for recurrence neural networks
